sources.title: CCR T14 §5.85(a)(2) — Trout, General Statewide Regulations (inland streams, rivers, and canals)
sources.quotedText: All inland streams, rivers, and canals, except those listed in Sections 7.40(b) and 7.50(b), are open to fishing from the last Saturday in April through November 15 with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit, with no gear restrictions. From November 16 through the Friday preceding the last Saturday in April, a zero-trout bag limit applies, and only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used for all species of fish. In waters where the bag limit for trout is zero, trout must be released unharmed and should not be removed from the water.

sources.title: CCR T14 §7.40 — Alphabetical List of Hatchery Trout, Hatchery Steelhead, and Salmon Waters with Special Fishing Regulations (Big Meadow Creek not listed)

CCR T14 §7.50 — Alphabetical List of Trout Waters with Special Fishing Regulations (Big Meadow Creek not listed; the Upper Truckee River special-regulation entry at §7.50(b)(161) covers only the reach and tributaries upstream from the confluence with Showers Creek, well upstream of Big Meadow Creek's confluence near Christmas Valley)
California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

sources.title: Upper Truckee River — Wikipedia
sources.quotedText: Tributaries include Angora, Echo, Grass Lake, and Big Meadow Creeks, and Upper and Lower Echo, Round, and Dardanelles Lakes.

sources.title: CA's Big Meadow Creek and Upper Truckee River, Section 319 Nonpoint Source Success Story — U.S. EPA
Big Meadow Trailhead — Lake Tahoe Basin Management Unit, U.S. Forest Service
Upper Truckee River (El Dorado Co.) — Tahoe Rim Trail approach via Big Meadow — SierraFish
sources.quotedText: Check out the plentiful Brook Trout in the stream while crossing the wooden bridge.

sources.title: Small Creek Fly-Fishing in the Tahoe Basin — Visit Lake Tahoe
sources.quotedText: Small creeks with names like Meeks, Blackwood, Trout, Eagle and Cascade all hold four- to ten-inch-long brook, brown and rainbow trout.

end.verbatim: Friday preceding the last Saturday in April

parameters.note: Big Meadow Creek is not listed in CCR §7.40(b) or §7.50(b) (the Upper Truckee River special-regulation entry at §7.50(b)(161) applies only upstream of the Showers Creek confluence, above Big Meadow Creek's own confluence near Christmas Valley), so it falls under the general statewide stream regulation in §5.85(a)(2).

regulations.humanSummary: Open from the last Saturday in April through November 15 (general statewide stream regulation; not listed as a special-regulation water); zero-trout, artificial-lures-with-barbless-hooks-only outside that window
regulations.verbatimText: All inland streams, rivers, and canals, except those listed in Sections 7.40(b) and 7.50(b), are open to fishing from the last Saturday in April through November 15 with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit, with no gear restrictions. From November 16 through the Friday preceding the last Saturday in April, a zero-trout bag limit applies, and only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used for all species of fish.

parameters.counting_rule: General statewide trout limit for inland streams, rivers, and canals during the open season (last Saturday in April through November 15): five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit, no gear restrictions.

regulations.humanSummary: 5 trout daily bag limit, 10 trout possession limit during the open season
regulations.verbatimText: All inland streams, rivers, and canals, except those listed in Sections 7.40(b) and 7.50(b), are open to fishing from the last Saturday in April through November 15 with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit, with no gear restrictions.

parameters.note: From November 16 through the Friday preceding the last Saturday in April, only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used, and the trout bag limit is zero (catch-and-release, fish must not be removed from the water).

regulations.humanSummary: Outside the general open season (Nov. 16 – Friday before last Saturday in April), artificial lures with barbless hooks only and a zero-trout bag limit
regulations.verbatimText: From November 16 through the Friday preceding the last Saturday in April, a zero-trout bag limit applies, and only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used for all species of fish. In waters where the bag limit for trout is zero, trout must be released unharmed and should not be removed from the water.

parameters.note: Anglers 16 years of age or older must have a valid California sport fishing license.

regulations.humanSummary: California sport fishing license required for anglers 16+
